title: null/undefined JS-distinct, no arithmetic
manager: FSL keeps `null` and `undefined` as distinct values — matching the JavaScript host rather than pretending the world is simpler than it is — and forbids arithmetic on either, so `null + 1` never quietly becomes 1. The two genuinely mean different things (explicitly-absent versus never-set), and conflating them or doing math on them is exactly where host-language bugs leak into machine logic and stay hidden until a guard reads wrong.
cs: null and undefined distinct, both first-class; arithmetic on either is a compile error; interacts with the .data undefined-setter fix (Tfsl-1264) and T? nullability (W6.25).
